name: Rubble
description: An unsightly heap of rubble fills this lot.
effects: Rubble is created accidentally, such as from a result of certain kingdom events or failed Demolish activity. You cannot build in a lot with rubble; it must be removed via a successful Demolish activity.

name: Tenement
description: Tenements are hastily built shanty-towns of tightly packed, multi-family dwellings that are cheap and fast to build.
ruin: +1 to a Ruin of your choice
effects: The first time you build tenements each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Brewery
description: A brewery is devoted to crafting alcohol, be it beer, wine, or spirits. This building can represent bottlers, vineyards, or even structures that produce nonalcoholic drinks.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Trade Agreement
effects: When you build a brewery, reduce Unrest by 1 as long as you have fewer than 4 breweries in the settlement at that time.

name: Cemetery
description: A cemetery sets aside a plot of land to bury the dead and can also include above-ground vaults or underground catacombs.
effects: If you have at least one cemetery in a settlement, reduce Unrest gained from any dangerous settlement events in that particular settlement by 1 (to a maximum of 4 for four cemeteries). The presence of a cemetery provides additional effects during certain kingdom events.

name: General Store
effects: A settlement without a general store or marketplace reduces its level for the purposes of determining what items can be purchased there by 2.

name: Granary
description: A granary consists of silos and warehouses for the storage of grain and other preserved foodstuffs.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Farmland
effects: Each granary in your kingdom increases your maximum Food Commodity capacity by 1.

name: Herbalist
description: An herbalist consists of small medicinal gardens tended by those with knowledge of herbs and their uses to heal or to harm, as well as a storefront for customers.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Provide Care
effects: A settlement with at least 1 herbalist treats its level as two levels higher than its actual level for the purposes of determining which common potions with the healing trait are readily available for sale in that settlement.

name: Houses
description: Houses provide a neighborhood of single and multi-family dwellings for your citizens.
effects: The first time you build houses each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Inn
description: An inn provides a safe and secure place for a settlement's visitors to rest.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Hire Adventurers and Clear Hex (Exploration)

name: Shrine
description: A shrine is a small building devoted to the worship of a deity or faith. It can be attended by resident priests or visiting clergy.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Celebrate Holiday
effects: A settlement with at least one shrine treats its level as one level higher than its actual level for the purposes of determining which common divine healing magic items are readily available for sale in that settlement. This increases to two levels higher for any common consumable magic items that meet the listed criteria.

name: Tavern, Dive
description: A dive tavern is a rough-and-tumble establishment for entertainment, eating, and drinking.
itemBonus: +1 to Clear Hex (Exploration)
effects: The first time you build a dive tavern in a Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1 but increase Crime by 1.

name: Wall, Wooden
description: Wooden walls provide serviceable defenses to a settlement.
effects: A wooden wall is built along the border of one of your settlement's Urban Grids. The first time you build a wooden wall in each settlement, reduce Unrest by 1. When you build a wooden wall, choose a border on your Urban Grid and check the appropriate checkbox. An Urban Grid may have up to 4 walls (wooden or stone), one on each side.

name: Bridge
description: Bridges give settlements that have water borders a connection to land (but at the GM's discretion, a border on a lake might not be able to use bridges).
effects: A bridge allows an island settlement to provide influence, negates the Trade penalty for island settlements, and allows travel over its associated Water Border with ease. Bridges can only be built on Water Borders. An Urban Grid may have a number of Bridges up to the number of Water Borders it has.

name: Dump
description: A dump is a centralized place for the disposal of refuse, often including a shack for a caretaker to live in.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Demolish
effects: Certain events have a more dangerous impact on settlements that don't include a dump. A dump cannot share a block with any Residential structures.

name: Jail
description: A jail is a fortified structure that houses criminals, prisoners, or dangerous monsters separate from the rest of society.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Quell Unrest using Intrigue
effects: The first time you build a jail each Kingdom turn, reduce Crime by 1.

name: Library
description: A library contains collections of books, scrolls, writings, and records conducive to research.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Creative Solution and Rest and Relax using Scholarship checks
effects: While in a settlement with a library, you gain a +1 item bonus to Lore checks made to Recall Knowledge while Investigating, as well as to Researching, and to Decipher Writing.

name: Mill
description: A mill grinds grain using the power of wind, water, or beasts of burden.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Harvest Crops
effects: If a settlement includes at least one mill built on a lot adjacent to a Water Border, the increased efficiency of these mills reduces the settlement's Consumption by 1 (to a minimum of 0).

name: Orphanage
description: This sprawling residential building provides housing for orphans or even homeless citizens, but it can also help supply housing for refugees.
effects: The first time you build an orphanage each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Town Hall
description: A town hall is a public venue for town meetings and a repository for town history and records.
construction: Defense (trained), Industry (trained), Magic (trained), or Statecraft (trained) DC 16
itemBonus: +1 to Manage Trade Agreements and Retrain
effects: A settlement with a Town Hall gains one settlement action per turn and increases the limit on Civic activities per turn in that settlement from one to two. If two Build Structure activities are taken within the settlement in a turn, at least one must be used to build a Structure with the Infrastructure trait. Unless the settlement is a village, it produces one Resource Die per Kingdom turn. The first time you build a town hall each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Alchemy Laboratory
description: An alchemy laboratory serves as a factory for alchemists and their apprentices for the crafting of potions, elixirs, and all manner of alchemical items.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Demolish
effects: A settlement with at least 1 alchemy laboratory treats its level as one level higher for determining which alchemical items are readily available for sale. This increases to two levels higher for any common consumable alchemical items. Checks to Identify Alchemy in any settlement with at least one alchemy laboratory gain a +1 item bonus.

name: Barracks
description: Barracks are focused on housing and training guards, militia, soldiers, and military forces.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Garrison Army, Recover Army, or Recruit Army
effects: Barracks aid in the recruitment of armies and in helping soldiers recover from battle. The first time you build a barracks in any settlement, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Festival Hall
description: A festival hall is a small building that gives performers a venue to entertain and citizens a place to gather for celebrations or simply to relax.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Celebrate Holiday, Quell Unrest (Arts) and Rest and Relax using Arts

name: Foundry
description: A foundry is a facility used to refine ore into finished metal.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Work Site (mine)
effects: Each foundry in your kingdom increases your maximum Ore Commodity capacity by 1. A foundry cannot share a block with a Residential structure.

name: Keep
description: A keep is a high-walled defensive structure that guards the heart of a settlement. It includes practice and marshaling yards as well as a refuge for your leaders should danger strike the settlement.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Deploy Army, Garrison Army, or Train Army
effects: The first time you build a keep each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Lumberyard
description: A lumberyard is an open area used to store additional lumber. The yard includes a lumber mill used to process lumber into timbers for construction purposes.
requirements: Must be built in a lot next to a water border to power its saws and facilitate the shipment of trees to the yard.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Work Site (lumber camp)
effects: Each lumberyard in your kingdom increases its maximum Lumber Commodity capacity by 1.

name: Monument
description: A monument is an impressive stone structure built to commemorate a historical event, honor a beloved leader, memorialize a tragedy, or simply serve as an artistic display.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Create a Masterpiece
effects: The first time you build a monument each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1 and reduce one Ruin of your choice by 1.

name: Park
description: A park is a plot of undeveloped land set aside for public use.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Rest and Relax using Wilderness checks
effects: The first time you build a park each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Pier
description: Several wooden piers allow easy access to fishing and provide a convenient place to moor boats.
requirements: Must be built in a lot next to a Water Border.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Go Fishing
effects: A settlement with at least 1 Pier reduces its Consumption by 1.

name: Planning Office
description: A planning office is an office inside a Civic structure set aside as a space to plan expansion.
requirements: The settlement has a structure with the Civic trait and is not a village.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Accelerate Project and Repair Reputation (Decay)
effects: The first time in a turn that Decay would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Decay gained by 1. This only applies to Leadership activities, Civic activities, and Region activities within the settlement's influence. A settlement may only have a single planning office and may not have both a planning office and planning department.

name: Publicity Office
description: A publicity office is an office inside a Civic Structure set aside as a space for the public to learn about government plans.
requirements: The settlement has a structure with the Civic trait and is not a village.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Repair Reputation (Strife)
effects: The first time in a turn that Strife would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Strife gained by 1. This only applies to Leadership activities, Civic activities, and Region activities within the settlement's influence. A settlement may only have a single publicity office and may not have both a publicity office and information department.

name: Smithy
description: A smithy consists of workshops and forges.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Trade Commodities and Clear Hex (Engineering), +1 item bonus to Outfit Army
effects: A settlement with at least 1 smithy treats its level as one level higher for determining which common armor, shields, and weapons are readily available for sale. While in a settlement with a smithy, you gain a +1 item bonus to Craft checks made to work with metal.

name: Stable
description: A stable consists of a yard and smaller structures to house, train, and sell mounts.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Trade Agreement

name: Stockyard
description: A stockyard includes several barns and pens used to house livestock and prepare them for slaughter.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Gather Livestock
effects: A settlement with at least one stockyard reduces its Consumption by 1.

name: Stonemason
description: A stonemason is a large building used to store and work quarried stone for preparation in building.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Work Site (Quarry)
effects: Each stonemason in your kingdom increases your maximum Stone Commodity capacity by 1.

name: Tannery
description: A tannery is a factory outfitted with racks, vats and tools for the preparation of hides and leather.
itemBonus: +1 to Trade Commodities
effects: A tannery cannot share a block with any Residential structure except tenements.

name: Tavern, Popular
description: A popular tavern is a respectable establishment for entertainment, eating, and drinking.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Hire Adventurers, Reconnoiter Hex, and to Rest and Relax using Trade
effects: The first time you build a popular tavern in a Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 2. If you attempt a Performance check to Earn Income in a settlement with a popular tavern, you gain a +1 item bonus. All checks made to Gather Information in a settlement with at least one popular tavern gain a +1 item bonus.

name: Town Square
description: A town square is a small outdoor space right outside a Civic building that can be used for meetings, performances, or other gatherings.
requirements: The settlement has a structure with the Civic trait and is not a village.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Repair Reputation (Corruption)
effects: The first time in a turn that Corruption would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Corruption gained by 1. This only applies to Leadership activities, Civic activities, and Region activities within the settlement's influence. A settlement may only have a single town square and may not have both a town square and public forum.

name: Town Watch
description: A town watch is a section of a Civic structure dedicated to organizing security.
requirements: The settlement has a structure with the Civic trait and is not a village.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Repair Reputation (Crime)
effects: The first time in a turn that Crime would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Crime gained by 1. This only applies to Leadership activities, Civic activities, and Region activities within the settlement's influence. A settlement may only have a single town watch and may not have both a town watch and city watch.

name: Trade Shop
description: A trade shop is a store that focuses on providing services.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Purchase Commodities
effects: When you build a trade shop, indicate the kind of shop it is (such as a bakery, carpenter, tailor, etc.). While in a settlement with a trade shop, you gain a +1 item bonus to all associated Crafting checks.

name: Watchtower
description: A watchtower serves as a guard post that grants a settlement advance warning to upcoming dangerous events.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to checks to resolve events affecting the settlement
effects: The first time you build a watchtower each Kingdom turn, decrease Unrest by 1.

name: Marketplace
description: A marketplace is a large neighborhood of shops run by local vendors around an open area for traveling merchants and farmers to peddle their wares.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Trade Agreement
effects: A settlement without a general store or marketplace reduces its level for determining what items can be purchased there by 2. A settlement with at least one marketplace treats its level as one level higher for determining which common, non-alchemical, non-magical items are readily available for sale. This increases to two levels higher for any consumable items that meet the listed criteria.

name: Paved Streets
description: Brick or cobblestone streets speed transportation and ease the passage of people, mounts, and vehicles.
effects: It takes a character only 5 minutes to move from one lot to an adjacent lot in an Urban Grid when moving on paved streets. When you build paved streets, check the paved streets checkbox on your Urban Grid.

name: Specialized Artisan
description: These shops and homes are devoted to crafters who create fine jewelry, glassware, clockworks, and the like.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Craft Luxuries
effects: While in a settlement with a specialized artisan, you gain a +1 item bonus to Craft checks made to craft specialized goods like jewelry.

name: Arcanist's Tower
description: An arcanist's tower is a home and laboratory for an arcane spellcaster (usually a wizard) and their apprentices, servants, and students.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Quell Unrest using Magic and Supernatural Solution
effects: A settlement with at least one arcanist's tower treats its level as one level higher for determining which common arcane magic items are readily available for sale. This increases to two levels higher for any consumable magic items. While in a settlement with an arcanist's tower, you gain a +1 item bonus to checks made to Borrow an Arcane Spell or Learn a Spell.

name: Bank
description: A bank is a secure building for storing valuables, granting loans, and collecting and transferring deposits.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Tap Treasury, Capital Investment, and Collect Taxes
effects: The Capital Investment Leadership activity can be used only in the capital or within the influence area of a settlement with a bank.

name: Fishing Fleets
description: A fleet of fishing boats supplies food for the settlement.
requirements: The settlement must contain a pier, waterfront, or both, and must have a hex containing a large body of water within its influence.
itemBonus: +1 Item bonus to Go Fishing and Rest and Relax using Boating
effects: A settlement containing a fishing fleet reduces its Consumption by 1 per fishing fleet (to a minimum of 0). A settlement may normally have only one fishing fleet. If the kingdom has expert proficiency in Boating and the settlement features both a waterfront and a second qualifying hex, it may build a second fishing fleet. At master proficiency and three qualifying hexes, this increases to three fishing fleets. Only one settlement may benefit from any individual hex for the purpose of fishing fleets.

name: Garrison
description: A garrison is a complex of barracks, training yards, and weapons storage and repair for maintaining your military.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Fortify Hex and +1 item bonus to Outfit Army or Train Army
effects: A garrison helps outfit armies with new gear or trains them. When you build a garrison, reduce Unrest by 1.

name: Guildhall
description: A guildhall serves as the headquarters for a trade guild or similar organization. It includes offices for its leaders and functionaries as well as workshops for its craftspeople and a storefront for customers.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Economy skill checks associated with the guildhall's specific trade focus
effects: When you build a guildhall, indicate what sort of organization it serves as headquarters for. While in a settlement with a guildhall, you gain a +1 item bonus to all related skill checks to Earn Income or to Repair.

name: Magical Streetlamps
description: Magical streetlamps are everburning torches fitted within lampposts along the streets.
effects: Magical streetlamps provide nighttime illumination for an entire Urban Grid. When you build magical streetlamps, check the magical streetlamps checkbox on your Urban Grid. The first time you build magical streetlamps in a Kingdom turn, reduce Crime by 1.

name: Mansion
description: This larger manor house houses a wealthy family.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Improve Lifestyle

name: Museum
description: A museum displays art, objects of important cultural note, wonders of the natural world, and other marvels in a place where citizens can observe and learn.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Rest and Relax using Arts
effects: A magic item of level 6 or higher that bears significant historical or regional value can be donated to a museum. Each time such an item is donated, reduce Unrest by 1. If that item is later removed from display, increase Unrest by 1.

name: Oddity Emporium
description: An oddity emporium is the home and shop of an occult practitioner, such as an astrologer, fortune-teller, harrower, or psychic.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Prognostication and Supernatural Solution
effects: A settlement with at least one oddity emporium treats its level as one level higher for determining which common occult magic items are readily available for sale. This increases to two levels higher for any consumable magic items, as well as for all common talismans. While in a settlement with an oddity emporium, you gain a +1 item bonus to Astronomy Lore and Fortune-Telling Lore checks.

name: Sacred Grove
description: This untouched land has been blessed by primal spirits, druids friendly with your settlement, or allied fey creatures.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Blessed Solution, Observe Customs, and Quell Unrest using Folklore
effects: A settlement with at least one sacred grove treats its level as one level higher for determining which common primal magic items are readily available for sale. This increases to two levels higher for any consumable magic items.

name: Temple
description: A temple is a building devoted to worshipping a deity or faith.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Blessed Solution, Celebrate Holiday, Observe Customs, and Provide Care
effects: A settlement with at least one temple treats its level as one level higher for determining which common divine magic items are readily available for sale. This increases to two levels higher for any consumable magic items. The first time you build a temple each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 2.

name: Thieves' Guild
description: The government knows this group exists but allows it to continue doing its business as long as the guild doesn't overstep its bounds.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Infiltration
effects: While in a settlement with a thieves' guild, you gain a +1 item bonus to Create Forgeries.

name: Wall, Stone
description: Stone walls provide solid defenses to a settlement's borders.
effects: A stone wall is built along the border of one of your settlement's Urban Grids. The first time you build a stone wall in each settlement, reduce Unrest by 1. An Urban Grid may have up to 4 walls (wooden or stone), one on each side.

name: Illicit Market
description: An illicit market uses a facade of shops and homes to cover the fact that unregulated and illegal trade takes place within its walls.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Clandestine Business
effects: A settlement with at least one illicit market treats its level as one level higher for determining what items are readily available for sale. This effect stacks with all other similar bonuses in the settlement.

name: Secure Warehouse
description: Secure warehouses are used to store valuables.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Craft Luxuries
effects: Each secure warehouse in your kingdom increases your maximum Luxuries Commodity capacity by 1.

name: Sewer
description: This underground sanitation system helps keep the settlement clean and disease-free.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Clandestine Business
effects: Each sewer system in a settlement reduces its Consumption by 1. Having a sewer system can also affect certain kingdom events. When you build a sewer system, check the sewer system checkbox on its Urban Grid.

name: Embassy
description: An embassy gives a place for diplomatic visitors to your kingdom to stay and bolsters international relations.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Send Diplomatic Envoy and Request Foreign Aid

name: Luxury Store
description: This collection of stores specializes in expensive, rare, and exotic goods that cater to the wealthy.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Establish Trade Agreement
effects: A luxury store must be built on a block that has either a mansion or a noble villa. A settlement with at least 1 luxury store reduces the RP cost when using Purchase Commodities to purchase Luxuries from 8 to 6 RP. A settlement with at least 1 luxury store treats its level as one level higher for determining which Uncommon and Rare items may be available for sale.

name: Magic Shop
description: These shops specialize in magic items and in connecting buyers with sellers of magical goods and services.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Prognostication and Supernatural Solution
effects: A settlement with at least 1 magic shop treats its level as one level higher for determining which common magic items are readily available for sale.

name: Waterfront
description: A waterfront serves as a bustling port for waterborne passengers and cargo, supported by facilities for shipping and shipbuilding, boardwalks for foot traffic and fishers.
requirements: Must be constructed next to a Water Border.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Go Fishing, and to Establish Trade Agreement and Rest and Relax using Boating
effects: A settlement with at least one waterfront increases its effective level by 1 for determining what level of items can be purchased; this bonus stacks with all other similar bonuses. The settlement also reduces its Consumption by 2 (this effect does not stack with the reduction provided by a pier).

name: Arena
description: An arena is a large public structure, traditionally open to the air, surrounded by seating and viewing areas. It's used for staging competitions, athletics, gladiatorial combats, and elaborate entertainments.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Celebrate Holiday and to Warfare checks made to Quell Unrest
effects: An arena lets you retrain combat-themed feats more efficiently while in the settlement; doing so takes only 5 days rather than a week of downtime.

name: Castle
description: A castle is a fortified structure that often serves as the seat of government for a kingdom.
construction: Defense (expert), Industry (expert), Magic (expert), or Statecraft (expert) DC 26
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Manage Trade Agreements, New Leadership, Pledge of Fealty, Relocate Capital, Retrain, and Send Diplomatic Envoy, and +2 item bonus to Garrison Army, Recover Army, or Recruit Army
effects: A settlement with a Castle gains two settlement actions per turn and increases the limit on Civic activities per turn from one to three. A town or city containing a castle produces one Resource Die per turn; a metropolis produces two. These are in addition to the one Resource Die a city or metropolis generates by itself. The first time you build a castle each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1d4.

name: City Watch
description: A city watch is a larger wing of a Civic structure dedicated to organizing security.
requirements: The settlement is a city or metropolis and has a structure with the Civic trait.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Repair Reputation (Crime)
effects: The first time in a turn that Crime would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Crime gained by 1. For each additional time Crime would be gained due to a critical failure, make a DC 17 flat check; on a success, reduce the Crime gained by 1. A settlement may only have a single City Watch.

name: Hospital
description: A hospital is a building dedicated to healing the sick through both magical and mundane means.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Provide Care and Quell Unrest
effects: While in a settlement with a hospital, you gain a +2 item bonus to Medicine checks to Treat Disease and Treat Wounds.

name: Information Department
description: An information department is a larger office inside a Civic structure set aside as a space for the public to learn about government plans.
requirements: The settlement is a city or metropolis and has a structure with the Civic trait.
itemBonus: +2 Item Bonus to Repair Reputation (Strife)
effects: The first time in a turn that Strife would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Strife gained by 1. For each additional time Strife would be gained due to a critical failure, make a DC 17 flat check; on a success, reduce the Strife gained by 1. A settlement may only have a single information department.

name: Noble Villa
description: This sprawling manor has luxurious grounds. It houses a noble family and their staff, and includes several smaller support structures such as servant's quarters, stables, and groundskeeper's cottages.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Improve Lifestyle and to Quell Unrest using Politics
effects: The first time you build a noble villa each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 2.

name: Planning Department
description: A larger office inside a Civic structure set aside as a space to plan expansion.
requirements: The settlement is a city or metropolis and has a structure with the Civic trait.
itemBonus: +2 Item Bonus to Accelerate Project and Repair Reputation (Decay)
effects: The first time in a turn that Decay would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Decay gained by 1. For each additional time Decay would be gained due to a critical failure, make a DC 17 flat check; on a success, reduce the Decay gained by 1. A settlement may only have a single planning department.

name: Public Forum
description: A public forum is a larger outdoor space right outside a Civic building that can be used for meetings, performances, or other gatherings.
requirements: The settlement is a city or metropolis and has a structure with the Civic trait.
itemBonus: +2 Item Bonus to Repair Reputation (Corruption)
effects: The first time in a turn that Corruption would be gained due to a critical failure, reduce the Corruption gained by 1. For each additional time Corruption would be gained due to a critical failure, make a DC 17 flat check; on a success, reduce the Corruption gained by 1. A settlement may only have a single public forum.

name: Tavern, Luxury
description: A luxury tavern is a high-class establishment for entertainment, eating, and drinking. It may even include a built-in stage for performers to use.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Hire Adventurers, Reconnoiter Hex, and to Rest and Relax using Trade
effects: The first time you build a luxury tavern in a Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1d4+1. If you attempt a Performance check to Earn Income in a settlement with a luxury tavern, you gain a +2 item bonus. All checks made to Gather Information in a settlement with at least one luxury tavern gain a +2 item bonus.

name: Theater
description: A theater is a venue for concerts, plays, and dances, but can double as a place for debates or other events.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Celebrate Holiday and Rest and Relax using Arts
effects: The first time you build a theater each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 1. While in a settlement with a theater, you gain a +2 item bonus to Performance checks made to Earn Income.

name: Academy
description: An academy gives your citizens an institution where advanced study in many fields can be pursued, researched, and referenced.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Creative Solution
effects: While in a settlement with an Academy, you gain a +2 item bonus to Lore checks made to Recall Knowledge while Investigating, to all checks made while Researching, and to Decipher Writing.

name: Construction Yard
description: A construction yard supports the building of structures by providing a centralized place to gather supplies and craft components for larger projects.
itemBonus: +1 item bonus to Accelerate Project, Build Roads, Build Structure, Irrigation, and Repair Reputation (Decay)
effects: A settlement with at least one construction yard increases the RP spending limit per turn on a given structure by 2. For structures with the Residential trait, the limit is increased by 3 instead. For structures with the Edifice trait, the limit is only increased by 1.

name: Menagerie
description: A menagerie is a large zoo that contains numerous enclosures, exhibits, tanks, or open preserves meant to display wildlife.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Rest and Relax using Wilderness
effects: A menagerie typically contains a selection of level 5 or lower animals. If your party captures a living creature of level 6 or higher, you can add it to the menagerie as long as your kingdom level is at least 4 higher than the creature's level. Each time such a creature is added, gain 1 Renown point or reduce one Ruin of your choice by 1. Only creatures with Intelligence modifiers of –4 or –5 are appropriate. A kingdom gains 1 Unrest at the start of a Kingdom turn for each sapient creature on display.

name: Military Academy
description: A military academy is dedicated to the study of war and the training of elite soldiers and officers.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Pledge of Fealty using Warfare, +2 item bonus to Train Army

name: Occult Shop
description: An occult shop is usually a sprawling, mysterious store that specializes in buying and selling obscure magic and strange curios. It often provides access to supernatural services like fortune-telling.
itemBonus: +2 item bonus to Supernatural Solution and Prognostication
effects: A settlement with at least one occult shop treats its level as one level higher for determining which common magic items are readily available for sale. A kingdom with at least one occult shop receives a free Supernatural Solution at the beginning of the Activity phase of each Kingdom turn (no RP cost). While in a settlement with an occult shop, you gain a +2 item bonus to all checks made to Research esoteric subjects or to Recall Knowledge about the same.

name: Cathedral
description: A cathedral serves as a focal point of spiritual worship in the settlement and the seat of regional power for a religion. Most cathedrals are astounding works of art and eye-catching marvels of architecture.
itemBonus: +3 item bonus to Blessed Solution, Celebrate Holiday, Observe Customs, Provide Care, and Repair Reputation (Corruption)
effects: A settlement with at least one cathedral treats its level as one level higher for determining which common divine magic items are readily available for sale. This increases to two levels higher for any consumable magic items. A kingdom with at least one cathedral receives a free Blessed Solution at the beginning of the Activity phase of each Kingdom turn (no RP cost), but must designate a specific kingdom activity for it. The first time you build a cathedral each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 4. While in a settlement with a cathedral, you gain a +3 item bonus to Lore and Religion checks made to Recall Knowledge while Investigating, and to all faith-themed checks made while Researching.

name: Mint
description: A mint allows the kingdom to produce its own coinage to augment its economy. It can also include fortified underground chambers to serve as a treasury.
itemBonus: +3 item bonus to Capital Investment, Collect Taxes, and to Repair Reputation (Crime)
effects: A kingdom with at least one mint does not suffer penalties from having used the Collect Taxes activity on the previous turn.

name: Opera House
description: An opera house functions well as a venue for operas, plays, and concerts, but also includes extensive facilities to aid in the training of all manner of bardic pursuits.
itemBonus: +3 item bonus to Celebrate Holiday, Create a Masterpiece, and to Rest and Relax using Arts
effects: A kingdom with at least one opera house gains 1 extra Renown at the start of the Activity phase of each Kingdom turn. The first time you build an opera house each Kingdom turn, reduce Unrest by 4. While in a settlement with an opera house, you gain a +3 item bonus to Performance checks made to Earn Income.

name: Palace
description: A palace is a grand and splendid seat of government for your leaders and other political functionaries.
construction: Defense (master), Industry (master), Magic (master), or Statecraft (master) DC 34
itemBonus: +3 item bonus to Manage Trade Agreements, New Leadership, Pledge of Fealty, Relocate Capital, Retrain, and Send Diplomatic Envoy, and +3 item bonus to Garrison Army, Recover Army, or Recruit Army
effects: A palace can only be built in your capital. A settlement with a palace gains two settlement actions per turn and increases the limit on Civic activities per turn from one to three. If the capital is a town or city, it produces one Resource Die per turn; a metropolis produces three (cumulative with the one Resource Die a city or metropolis generates by itself). A PC in the Ruler leadership role gains a +3 item bonus to checks made to resolve Leadership activities. The first time you build a palace, reduce Unrest by 10.

name: Tavern, World-Class
description: A World-Class Tavern is a legendary establishment for entertainment, eating, and drinking. It has at least one venue for performances—perhaps multiple ones.
itemBonus: +3 item bonus to Hire Adventurers, to Rest and Relax using Trade, to Reconnoiter Hex, and to Repair Reputation (Strife)
effects: A kingdom with at least one world-class tavern reduces Unrest by 1 at the start of the Activity phase of each kingdom turn. The first time you build a world-class tavern in a turn, reduce Unrest by 2d4. If you try a Performance check to Earn Income in a settlement with a world-class tavern, you gain a +3 item bonus. All checks made to Gather Information in a settlement with a world-class tavern gain a +3 item bonus.

name: University
description: A university is a sprawling institution of higher learning.
itemBonus: +3 item bonus to Creative Solution
effects: A kingdom with at least one university receives a free Creative Solution at the beginning of the Activity phase of each Kingdom turn (no RP cost). While in a settlement with a university, you gain a +3 item bonus to Lore checks made to Recall Knowledge while Investigating, to Research checks, and to Decipher Writing.
